commands.review_summary:
The PR makes Slack progress-mode draft boundaries a no-op so multi-segment assistant turns keep one editable progress draft instead of forcing a new draft message at each assistant/reasoning boundary.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main sets hasStreamedMessage from progress draft updates, then assistant/reasoning boundaries can clear the Slack draft message id before the next update. I did not run a live Slack reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real Slack behavior proof, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(slack): keep one draft message in progress mode
target.body:
## Summary

In `channels.slack.streaming.mode = "progress"` (internally `status_final`) the Slack draft preview is supposed to be a single rolling message that gets finalised in place by `finalizeSlackPreviewEdit` when the final reply lands. In practice, multi-segment assistant turns (text → tool → text → tool → text) ended up posting **one new draft message per assistant segment**, each individually edited, with the final reply edited into only the *last* one — leaving the earlier drafts as orphaned progress messages in the channel.

## Root cause

`onDraftBoundary` is wired to every `onAssistantMessageStart` and `onReasoningEnd` event in `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts`. The boundary unconditionally calls `draftStream.forceNewMessage()` — which clears `messageId` so the next `draftStream.update()` falls back to `chat.postMessage` instead of editing the existing preview.

That behaviour is correct for `partial` / `block` modes where each assistant segment is its own delivered chunk. In `status_final` it actively breaks the single-rolling-preview contract.

## Fix

Make `onDraftBoundary` a no-op in `status_final` mode. Other modes are unchanged.

```ts
const onDraftBoundary = !shouldUseDraftStream
  ? undefined
  : async () => {
      if (streamMode === "status_final") {
        return;                          // <-- new
      }
      if (hasStreamedMessage) {
        draftStream?.forceNewMessage();
        ...
      }
      ...
    };
```

## Tests

Two new cases in `dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`:

1. **Regression**: in progress mode, `forceNewMessage` is **never** called even when `onAssistantMessageStart` / `onReasoningEnd` fire mid-turn.
2. **Sanity**: in `partial`/`replace` mode, `forceNewMessage` is still called on the assistant boundary so we haven't silenced non-progress modes.

All 67 tests in `dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` + `dispatch.streaming.test.ts` pass.

## How we found it

Production Slack bot was posting multiple progress messages per turn instead of one editable preview. Traced through `pipeline.runtime` / `dispatch.ts` to the unconditional `forceNewMessage()` call on the assistant boundary.
target.author: mycarrysun
